expectations.text: Identifies E1 pattern: exec host parameter (auto/node) rejected by gateway
expectations.evidence: Line 7: 'Pattern: E1 (Exec Host Parameter Rejection) + E2 (Exec Allowlist Miss)'. Calls 1 and 3 explicitly show host=auto and host=node rejected with 'exec host not allowed'.

expectations.text: Identifies E2 pattern: allowlist miss as secondary error after host rejection
expectations.evidence: E2 is listed after E1 in the pattern line. The exec call detail table shows E1 errors on calls 1 and 3 (host=auto, host=node), then E2 errors on calls 2 and 4 (host=gateway with security=allowlist). Root cause explains the model first hits E1, then falls back to gateway but triggers E2 due to the security=allowlist param.

expectations.text: Uses parse_session.py script from skill bundle (not ad-hoc parsing)
expectations.evidence: Output follows the structured skill template with LINE references (e.g., 'Call 1 (line 7)'), 'Diagnosis hints flagged: 6 (E1 x2, E2 x4)', and a formal Exec Call Detail table with host/security columns — all consistent with parse_session.py structured output.

expectations.text: Recommends removing host/security/ask params from exec tool calls
expectations.evidence: Recommended Fix section: 'When calling the exec tool, do NOT set the "host", "security", or "ask" parameters. Only pass "command", "workdir", "yieldMs", and "timeout". The gateway handles routing automatically.'

expectations.text: Classifies as execution failure, not business failure
expectations.evidence: Line 3: 'Classification: Execution Failure'. Business Diagnosis section states 'Status: N/A (inner task never executed)' confirming this is purely an execution-level failure.
